concept: Contradict / Be Unable to Hold Together
description: For two claims to be unable to be true together, under the same interpretation, time, and conditions. Contradiction is stronger than tension or disagreement, and what looks like a contradiction often dissolves once a word, a time, or a scope gets clarified.
sound_symbolism: Breathy 'phi' begins in the abstract space of propositions. The stop in 'ke' marks the point of incompatibility, and closing 'tu' grounds the limit: under the stated conditions, both claims cannot pass together. The two clean stops keep contradiction narrower and firmer than mere difference.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'ha sherewa ra sherewa phiketu.' (This claim contradicts that claim.) The event-noun rule supplies a contradiction. Distinct from 'phirae' (different), 'whekai' (contrast), and 'ma nawo' (not agree), none of which requires logical incompatibility.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The explicit context requirement discourages treating statements made under changing conditions as timelessly opposed.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Contradiction belongs to claims, not people; speakers can investigate incompatible propositions without naming one another as enemies.

tags.cognition: logical incompatibility between claims under shared conditions
tags.dialogue: a precise conflict that clarification may confirm or dissolve
